description_long: This object is a square section of dark asphalt pavement featuring a large, white International Symbol of Access, commonly known as the wheelchair symbol, painted prominently in the center. The symbol consists of a stylized human figure seated in a wheelchair, with a circular wheel and a simple, geometric body and head. The paint appears slightly weathered, suggesting exposure to outdoor conditions and regular use. The surface of the pavement is rough and granular, typical of asphalt, with the paint providing a contrasting smoothness and brightness. There are no physical three-dimensional wheelchair or person components; the entire depiction is flat and integrated into the pavement. This marking is typically found in parking lots, sidewalks, or other urban environments to designate accessible spaces for wheelchair users.
description: A flat, square section of dark asphalt pavement with a large, white painted wheelchair accessibility symbol in the center. The surface is rough and the symbol is clearly visible, indicating an accessible area.
description_view_0: The wheelchair symbol is clearly visible, with the figure facing right and the paint appearing slightly worn on the dark pavement.
description_view_1: The symbol is seen from a lower angle, emphasizing the flatness of the surface and the contrast between the white paint and the dark asphalt.
description_view_2: The wheelchair symbol is viewed from the opposite side, with the figure facing left, and the rough texture of the pavement is evident.
description_view_3: A direct view of the symbol, showing the full wheelchair figure and the circular wheel, with the paint appearing most intact.
attribute_wheel_configuration: no physical wheels present; symbol only
attribute_seating_posture: A stylized figure is depicted upright with arms and legs extended in a simple geometric form.
